skill.name: ai-music
skill.description: Routes music generation and editing requests to RunComfy models through the runcomfy CLI, including ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, inpaint, and outpaint workflows.
skill.summary: Generate, repair, and extend AI music with RunComfy model routing.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick, URL, and hidden-file patterns, but review found a Markdown-only skill rather than executable Ruby or embedded scripts. The confirmed risks are legitimate external runcomfy CLI execution, outbound RunComfy API use, and documented token storage, so publication is reasonable with a marketplace warning.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access for runcomfy commands and instructs the agent to run model jobs through the CLI. This is an intended capability, but it executes a local external command and sends prompts or audio references to a remote service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool declaration and command examples directly confirm external CLI use. The surrounding text frames it as the core feature, not as hidden or malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Authentication Token Stored by CLI
medium_findings.description: The security notes state that runcomfy login writes an API token under the user config directory. This is normal CLI behavior, but it creates a local secret that users must protect.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly documents token storage and permissions. I found no evidence that the skill reads or exfiltrates the token itself.

medium_findings.title: User Audio and Prompts Leave the Local Environment
medium_findings.description: The skill sends generation prompts and user-provided audio URLs to RunComfy model endpoints. This is necessary for the service, but users should avoid sensitive or unlicensed content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples include audio URL inputs and the privacy section describes HTTPS transmission to the Model API. The skill also documents mitigations for untrusted source audio.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Formatting Triggered Static Backtick Alerts
low_findings.description: Most static external-command detections are inline code spans or fenced shell examples in SKILL.md. They are not Ruby backtick execution or executable source code, although several examples show intended runcomfy commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a Markdown file and the flagged regions are code fences or inline model identifiers. There is no Ruby source file or automatic shell evaluation path in the skill package.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Service Links
low_findings.description: Static network findings point to RunComfy documentation, model pages, and example user CDN URLs. These links match the advertised service and do not show hidden callbacks or unrelated endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible user-facing references or endpoint documentation for the named service. I did not find evidence of covert telemetry or credential exfiltration URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance locations refer to descriptive text, model-routing guidance, or an exit-code table. No cryptographic operation or host reconnaissance command is present in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not contain crypto APIs, hashing, or environment inspection commands. The detections appear to come from keyword matches in prose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Pattern for runcomfy Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits Bash(runcomfy *) and uses runcomfy run examples. This should remain constrained to runcomfy commands and should not be expanded to arbitrary shell access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is explicit in the tool declaration and examples. It is constrained to the named CLI, which lowers the severity but does not remove operational risk.

content.value_statement: Music generation requests often require choosing between quality, price, language support, and editing needs. This skill routes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to the right RunComfy music workflow with practical prompts and CLI commands.
actual_capabilities: Selects between ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, ACE Step 1.5, audio-inpaint, and audio-outpaint routes.
Provides runcomfy CLI invocation patterns for text-to-music generation.
Guides prompt structure for vocals, instrumentals, sections, tags, lyrics, and duration.
Explains when to use premium models versus lower-cost batch generation.
Supports repair and extension workflows for existing audio with time ranges or added sections.
Documents RunComfy authentication, output handling, and relevant exit codes.
limitations: Requires the runcomfy CLI and a valid RunComfy account or token.
Generated audio quality depends on the selected model and prompt detail.
Existing audio edits require user-provided audio URLs and supported time ranges.
The skill does not verify copyright ownership for lyrics, melodies, or source audio.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Music
use_cases.description: Produce short premium jingles or polished vocal hooks for ads and launches.

use_cases.title: Build Background Libraries
use_cases.description: Draft many affordable instrumental tracks for videos, games, or social content.

use_cases.title: Repair Existing Tracks
use_cases.description: Regenerate a weak section or extend a hook into a longer arrangement.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Instrumental
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60 second instrumental background track for a product demo. Use a warm electronic style, steady tempo, and no vocals.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Vocal Song Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured request with style and lyrics.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 90 second indie pop song with female vocals. Include verse and chorus lyrics, clear section markers, and a bright but not aggressive mix.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Cost Versus Quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow where model choice matters.
prompt_templates.prompt: I need 30 draft tracks for a meditation app. Optimize for low cost, calm variation, and consistent 60 second lengths. Pick the best RunComfy route.

prompt_templates.title: Extend and Repair Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this audio URL to extend the track with a 20 second intro and 45 second outro. Keep the same style and avoid adding vocals.

output_examples.input: Generate a short brand jingle with vocals.
output_examples.output: The skill selects ElevenLabs Music, drafts a sectioned prompt with style and lyrics, sets a short duration, and returns the runcomfy command to render the audio.
The skill selects ACE Step, builds varied tag sets, recommends duration controls, and explains the lower-cost batch workflow.

output_examples.input: Fix the second chorus in an existing song.
output_examples.output: The skill selects ACE Step audio-inpaint, asks for the audio URL and time range, then prepares the edit request with matching style tags.

best_practices: State the desired use, duration, vocal needs, language, and quality target before generation.
Use short drafts for iteration, then render longer versions after the direction is approved.
Keep API tokens out of prompts, logs, repositories, and shared project files.
anti_patterns: Do not use copyrighted lyrics or source audio unless you have the required rights.
Do not pass private audio, confidential campaigns, or sensitive prompts to remote models without approval.
Do not expand the allowed Bash scope beyond runcomfy commands for this skill.

faq.question: Does this skill generate music locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the runcomfy CLI to send requests to RunComfy model endpoints.

faq.question: Which models does it route to?
faq.answer: It routes to ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, ACE Step 1.5, audio-inpaint, and audio-outpaint workflows.

faq.question: Can it make instrumental tracks?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes prompt patterns for instrumental music and model settings that avoid vocals.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing song?
faq.answer: Yes, for supported ACE Step workflows. It can regenerate a time range or extend audio before and after.

faq.question: Does it handle commercial rights?
faq.answer: No. Users are responsible for rights to lyrics, source audio, prompts, and final outputs.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Review model terms, RunComfy costs, source material rights, and whether remote processing is allowed for the project.
